target: LOCOMO dataset ground truth and structural errors
method: Each agent independently read the full dataset (1280 lines) and existing evaluation, then performed exhaustive edge-walk verification. Results compared below.

original_15_errors_verification.summary: All 15 original errors verified by all 3 agents. Zero refutations.
unanimous_confirmed: GT-1: Missing Memory[58] from Query 5 expected (4th Decision in session 4)
GT-2: Query 9 expected [30,31] should include all 10 session-3 bugs — CRITICAL
GT-3: Query 10 missing Memory[49] (second timeline-impact memory)
GT-5: Query 13 missing Memory[4] (original testing strategy, author's own comment notes it)
QT-1: Query 2 'What database?' is multi_hop (expected size=2 contradicts single_hop definition)
QT-2: Query 9 is temporal_multi_hop, not pure temporal
ABS-1: Query 9 missing Memory[48] (session-4 bugs) from absence list
ABS-2: Query 6 missing session-1 memories from absence list
CARD-1: Expected set size asymmetry (single_hop 1.2 vs temporal 2.2 mean)
TAG-1: Person-name tags inconsistently applied across corpus
TAG-2: Redundant 'decision' tag on Memory[2,3] creates double-boosting
TYPE-1: Memory[5] milestones better typed as Decision than Task

confirmed_with_nuance.claim: Query 12 missing Memory[18] (original decision for reversal context)
confirmed_with_nuance.haiku: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — Memory[18] is context, not direct answer to 'reversed'
confirmed_with_nuance.sonnet: CONFIRMED with nuance — argues internal inconsistency: if [18] needed for [46] reversal, then [47] needed for [48] reversal, but neither is included. Either both originals or neither.
confirmed_with_nuance.opus: CONFIRMED — the 'why' of reversal is semantically incomplete without original. MRR unaffected.
confirmed_with_nuance.consensus: CONFIRMED MEDIUM — adding [18] improves answer completeness but isn't strictly required. Sonnet's internal consistency argument (GT-9) is the strongest framing.

confirmed_with_nuance.claim: Memory[38] (CRDT optimization) should be Error not Learning
confirmed_with_nuance.haiku_round2: CONFIRMED — cross-checked against session 3 peers, Error pattern matches
confirmed_with_nuance.sonnet: CONFIRMED — compared with Memory[44] (also Task, same pattern), notes inconsistency
confirmed_with_nuance.opus: CONFIRMED — all other problem+fix memories in session 3 are Error type
confirmed_with_nuance.consensus: CONFIRMED LOW — Haiku initially resisted but confirmed on deeper analysis

found_by_all_3.description: count_absence_violations checks all 10 returned results but MRR/R@5/P@5 evaluate top-5. Absence violation at rank 8 penalized equally to rank 1. Asymmetric scoring.
found_by_all_3.haiku: CONFIRMED — identified as systematic scoring asymmetry
found_by_all_3.sonnet: CONFIRMED (SCORE-4) — notes bad results in 6-10 penalized but good results only get R@10 credit
found_by_all_3.opus: CONFIRMED (F20/F21) — notes absence violations are not position-weighted

found_by_all_3.description: Query 6 'What happened during the second meeting?' expected [15,17,18] is 3 of 15 valid session-2 memories. Same structural problem as GT-2.
found_by_all_3.haiku: CONFIRMED — identified as GT-2-scale issue for Query 6
found_by_all_3.sonnet: CONFIRMED (GT-8) — notes Memory[24] (timeline impact) specifically missing
found_by_all_3.opus: CONFIRMED (F12) — system returning [16,19,20,22,25] gets MRR=0.0 despite all being correct

found_by_all_3.description: Query 16 'What are the biggest risks?' expected [11] only (session-1 risk assessment). Memory[55] (tech debt, session 4) contains more current risk content. Session-1 risks like 'CRDT complexity' already resolved.
found_by_all_3.haiku: CONFIRMED — noted absence list too sparse for this query
found_by_all_3.sonnet: CONFIRMED (GT-11) — system surfacing [55] as rank-1 gets MRR=0.0 for a more current answer
found_by_all_3.opus: CONFIRMED (F14) — 'biggest risks' at current project state should include realized risks

found_by_all_3.description: print_report separator widths inconsistent: line 1096 uses 78 chars, line 1110 uses 88 chars. Cosmetic table misalignment.

found_by_2_of_3.description: Query 15 'Summarize the project' expected [0,45,49] missing Memory[59] (lessons learned summary, importance 0.85). A canonical project synthesis.
Query 19 'What patterns in decision-making?' missing Memory[51] which explicitly says 'decisions made too quickly without enough evaluation'. Direct pattern description.

Query 5 'What decisions last week?' absence [0,2,3] only excludes session-1. Session 2/3 Decision memories (e.g. Memory[15] importance 0.90, Memory[18] importance 0.95) unguarded — could surface for 'last week' with no penalty.
found_by_2_of_3.sonnet: CONFIRMED (GT-7 and ABS-5 — calls Memory[15] the highest-importance unguarded memory, rates ABS-5 as HIGH)

found_by_2_of_3.description: Memory[44] (encryption at rest) typed Task but describes completed implementation with measured results ('~2ms latency'). Better as Observation or Learning.
Memory[9] (infrastructure plan) typed Task but Memory[22] (observability stack, same pattern: 'Finalized X') typed Decision. Inconsistent Task/Decision boundary.

Composite weights MRR at 30%. A system that puts 1 correct result at rank-1 + 4 irrelevant gets MRR=1.0, P@5=0.2 → composite contribution 0.34. A system with 3 correct at ranks 2-4 gets MRR=0.5, P@5=0.6 → contribution 0.27. Rewards rank-1 gambling over broad quality.
found_by_2_of_3.sonnet: CONFIRMED (STRUCT-2 — frames as R@10 incentive interaction)

found_by_2_of_3.description: recall_at_k returns 1.0 for empty relevant set, reciprocal_rank returns 0.0, precision_at_k returns 0.0. Inconsistent empty-set semantics (latent, no current impact).
If GT-2 is fixed (expected expanded to 10 items), R@5 can never exceed 0.5 for that query since max 5 of 10 expected can appear in top-5. Fix recommendation interacts with metric design.

found_by_1_of_3_only.description: Memory[46] says 'moving workspace analytics and audit logs BACK to PostgreSQL' but no prior memory establishes analytics ever left PostgreSQL. Memory[18] only moves document storage to MongoDB. The 'back to' phrasing implies analytics was in MongoDB, but this was never recorded.

Memory[21] has ExperienceType::Conversation but content says 'Net assessment: keeping Rust because...' — this IS a decision (reconfirming Rust). Conversation type with 'decision' tag creates opposite problem from TAG-2.
Memory[53] is the ONLY Discovery-type memory in the entire corpus. Makes Discovery type untestable as a category for type-based boosting.

Query 11 'Who proposed the architecture that caused the most bugs?' — 'most bugs' attribution is ambiguous. CRDT decision (Memory[15]) arguably caused more bugs (30,34,38,40) than microservice split (Memory[48] lists 3). Expected [47,48] is defensible but ambiguous.

Memory[48] typed Error but contains 'Decided to revert to the monolith' — explicit decision embedded in Error-type memory. Content-based retrieval for Query 5 ('decisions last week') could surface this without type-boost.

Memory[46] appears in 3 expected sets (Q5,Q8,Q12). Memory[48] in 3 (Q8,Q11,Q12). Memory[18] in 3 (Q2,Q7,Q10). Pipeline changes affecting these memories create correlated improvements/regressions across queries, inflating apparent effect size.

With N=5 per category and MRR std dev ~0.4, 95% CI on category MRR is +/-0.35. Category-level differences under 0.35 are NOT statistically significant. The 0.233 gap between single_hop (0.500) and multi_hop (1.000) MRR barely exceeds significance threshold. The benchmark's N is too small for reliable per-category comparisons.
Memory[46] tags 'reversed', Memory[48] tags 'revert'. Stemming may not unify these for Query 12 entity overlap. Inconsistent tag vocabulary for the same concept.
Line 1037: latency averaging casts through f32 → u64, losing fractional milliseconds. Informational only.

found_by_copilot_source_audit.description: Query 2 'What database are we using?' expected [3,18] is incomplete for current-state retrieval because Memory[46] later moves analytics and audit logs back to PostgreSQL. A system returning both MongoDB and PostgreSQL evidence can be scored as wrong.

Query 11 'Who proposed the architecture that caused the most bugs?' encodes a contestable premise. The fixture hard-codes the microservice split [47,48], but the corpus also supports a broader debugging-phase bug cluster as the dominant bug source, so the query is not cleanly testable as written.
precision_at_k always divides by k even when fewer than k results are returned. This is a valid fixed-denominator variant, but it is undocumented and changes how sparse result sets are interpreted.

The benchmark contract depends on positional memory indices throughout the fixture. Any insertion or reorder in the corpus invalidates expected sets across the test, making the dataset brittle to maintenance changes.

The open-domain block is described as broad and context-dependent, but is graded against narrow exact index sets. This under-rewards semantically valid alternative summaries and makes synthesis prompts behave like exact-match fixtures.

Type-level reporting publishes only means for MRR, recall, precision, and latency. It exposes no variance or spread, so one or two catastrophic outliers can be hidden behind stable-looking category averages.

independent_source_review.summary: Single-agent source-only audit of tests/locomo_benchmark.rs completed without consulting this JSON before review. The audit confirms multiple existing findings directly from the Rust fixture and scoring code, and adds source-derived structural issues not explicitly captured in the 3-agent report.
independent_source_review.method: Read the benchmark source directly, reviewed corpus entries, query fixtures, and scoring helpers, then mapped findings into the same reporting style after the independent audit was complete.

confirmed_existing_findings.description: Query 'What decisions did we make last week?' still omits Memory[58] from the expected set.

evidence.snippet: query: "What decisions did we make last week?" ... expected_memory_indices: vec![46, 49, 52]

confirmed_existing_findings.description: Query 'What bugs did we find during the debugging phase?' still expects only [30,31], despite the debugging-phase block containing a much wider bug/fix cluster.

evidence.snippet: query: "What bugs did we find during the debugging phase?" ... expected_memory_indices: vec![30, 31]

confirmed_existing_findings.description: The testing-strategy change query comment mentions the original testing memory but the expected set still omits Memory[4].

evidence.snippet: // Original testing (4), Sarah's testing value (35), evolution (43) ... expected_memory_indices: vec![35, 43]

confirmed_existing_findings.description: Query 9 absence list still omits Memory[48].

evidence.snippet: absence_indices: vec![0, 5, 14]

confirmed_existing_findings.description: Query 'What happened during the second meeting?' still lacks explicit session-1 exclusions in its absence list.

evidence.snippet: query: "What happened during the second meeting?" ... absence_indices: vec![30, 45, 48]

confirmed_existing_findings.description: The composite-score comment and the implemented weight formula still disagree.

evidence.snippet: 40% MRR + 30% Recall@5 + 20% Precision@5 + 10% absence compliance ... 0.30 * overall_mrr + 0.20 * overall_recall + 0.15 * overall_recall_10 + 0.20 * overall_precision + 0.15 * absence_compliance

confirmed_existing_findings.description: The second-meeting temporal query still expects only [15,17,18].

evidence.snippet: expected_memory_indices: vec![15, 17, 18]

confirmed_existing_findings.description: The project-summary query still omits Memory[59] despite Memory[59] being an explicit lessons-learned synthesis.

evidence.snippet: query: "Summarize the project so far" ... expected_memory_indices: vec![0, 45, 49]
// 59: Lessons learned summary ... content: "Key lessons learned: invest heavily in property-based testing ..."

confirmed_existing_findings.description: The risk-summary query still expects only Memory[11].

evidence.snippet: query: "What are the biggest risks?" ... expected_memory_indices: vec![11]

confirmed_existing_findings.description: The decision-pattern query still excludes the explicit retrospective pattern memory.

evidence.snippet: query: "What patterns do you see in our decision-making?" ... expected_memory_indices: vec![52, 59]

source_only_findings.description: The database query expected set is incomplete for current-state retrieval. It expects [3,18] but omits Memory[46], which explicitly moves analytics and audit logs back to PostgreSQL.

evidence.snippet: query: "What database are we using?" ... expected_memory_indices: vec![3, 18]
Reversed part of the MongoDB decision: moving workspace analytics and audit logs back to PostgreSQL

source_only_findings.impact: A system returning both MongoDB and PostgreSQL evidence for the current architecture can be scored as wrong.

source_only_findings.description: The query 'Who proposed the architecture that caused the most bugs?' bakes in a contestable premise. The expected answer hard-codes the microservice split, but the corpus contains a larger debugging-phase bug cluster unrelated to that architecture.

evidence.snippet: query: "Who proposed the architecture that caused the most bugs?" ... expected_memory_indices: vec![47, 48]
query: "What bugs did we find during the debugging phase?" ... expected_memory_indices: vec![30, 31]
The microservice split prototype exposed several bugs

source_only_findings.impact: A model that infers a different architecture as the dominant bug source can be penalized despite the corpus not cleanly supporting the fixture premise.

source_only_findings.description: precision_at_k always divides by k, even if fewer than k results are returned.

evidence.snippet: fn precision_at_k(...) ... let hits = top_k.iter().filter(|r| relevant.contains(r)).count(); hits as f32 / k as f32

source_only_findings.impact: The metric semantics are fixed-denominator Precision@k, which is defensible but should be documented because it changes how sparse result sets are interpreted.

source_only_findings.description: The benchmark relies on positional memory indices as the fixture contract. Any insertion or reorder in the corpus invalidates expected sets across the test.

evidence.snippet: // 0: Project initiation
expected_memory_indices: vec![...] is used throughout the query fixture

source_only_findings.impact: The dataset is brittle to maintenance changes and mixes content truth with physical ordering.

source_only_findings.description: The open-domain block describes broad, context-dependent prompts but still grades against narrow exact index sets.

evidence.snippet: // OPEN-DOMAIN: Broad, context-dependent queries ... expected_memory_indices: vec![0, 45, 49] / vec![11] / vec![52, 59]

source_only_findings.impact: This turns broad synthesis prompts into brittle exact-match tests and under-rewards semantically valid alternative summaries.

source_only_findings.description: Type-level reporting averages MRR/Recall/Precision but exposes no variance, spread, or per-type instability signal.

evidence.snippet: TypeSummary { ... mrr: matching.iter().map(|r| r.mrr).sum::<f32>() / nf, ... avg_latency_ms: (...) as u64 }

source_only_findings.impact: Category summaries can look stable while hiding one or two catastrophic outliers.

CRITICAL: GT-2: Query 9 expected [30,31] remains severely under-scoped in source.
SRC-Q11-PREMISE-ERROR: The 'most bugs' architecture query encodes a contestable premise.
HIGH: SCORE-1: Composite-score comment still disagrees with the implemented formula.
SRC-Q2-CURRENT-STATE: Query 2 omits Memory[46], so the current database state is under-specified.
MEDIUM: ABS-1: Query 9 absence list still omits Memory[48].
ABS-2: Query 6 still lacks explicit session-1 exclusions.
NEW-Q6-NARROW: The second-meeting query still expects only [15,17,18].
NEW-Q15-MEMORY59: The project-summary query still omits Memory[59].
NEW-Q16-STALE: The risk query still expects only Memory[11].
SRC-PRECISION-K-FIXED-DENOM: Precision@k semantics are fixed-denominator and undocumented.
SRC-INDEX-BRITTLENESS: The benchmark contract depends on positional indices.
SRC-OPEN-DOMAIN-EXACTNESS: Broad prompts are still evaluated as narrow exact-set fixtures.
LOW: GT-5: The testing-strategy query comment still mentions Memory[4] while omitting it from the expected set.
NEW-Q19-MEMORY51: The decision-pattern query still omits the explicit retrospective-pattern memory.
SRC-TYPE-SUMMARY-NO-VARIANCE: Type-level reporting still publishes means without spread.

total_findings.sonnet: 21 findings (15 known + 6 medium new + 15 low/info new)

unique_strengths.haiku: Fast verification, caught all original errors. Weakest at finding new issues — only 4 unique new findings. Tends to confirm rather than discover.
unique_strengths.sonnet: Found the most semantically subtle errors: corpus inconsistency (Memory[46] 'back to' PostgreSQL with no established departure), Memory[21] type/tag contradiction, and the R@5 ceiling interaction with GT-2 fix. Strongest at cross-referencing within the corpus narrative.
unique_strengths.opus: Most exhaustive — 34 total findings. Found statistical significance bounds (CI +/-0.35), MRR-gambling incentive, correlated memory effects, and the Memory[53] singleton type issue. Strongest at structural/systemic analysis. Only agent to provide exact statistical significance calculations.
unique_strengths.copilot_source_audit: Strongest at source-grounded benchmark-contract review. Added current-state ambiguity for Query 2, elevated Query 11 from ambiguity to flawed benchmark premise, and surfaced design-level issues around fixed-denominator Precision@k, positional-index brittleness, exact-set grading for open-domain prompts, and mean-only category summaries.

haiku_missed: Query 5 absence gap for session 2/3 decisions
Corpus inconsistency in Memory[46] 'back to' phrasing
R@5 ceiling interaction with GT-2 fix
opus_missed: Query 5 session 2/3 Decision memories unguarded in absence (Sonnet's ABS-5)
R@5 ceiling interaction with GT-2 fix (Sonnet's SCORE-3)
Empty-set handling inconsistency across scoring functions
copilot_source_audit_missed: Did not independently validate the CI significance calculation from source alone
Did not independently elevate Memory[44], Memory[9], or Memory[53] type issues as primary source-only additions
Did not independently promote tag-vocabulary mismatches beyond what the benchmark contract itself makes test-relevant

CRITICAL: GT-2: Query 9 expected [30,31] should include all 10 session-3 bugs. 3/3 agents confirmed.
HIGH: QT-1: Query 2 is multi_hop not single_hop. 3/3 confirmed.
SCORE-1: Comment/code weight mismatch. 3/3 confirmed (downgraded to MEDIUM by adversarial).
SRC-Q2-CURRENT-STATE: Query 2 expected [3,18] omits Memory[46], so the current database state is under-specified. Copilot source audit.
SRC-Q11-PREMISE-ERROR: Query 11 encodes a contestable benchmark premise about which architecture caused the most bugs. Copilot source audit.
NEW-CI-BOUNDS: N=5/category gives CI +/-0.35 on MRR — category comparisons unreliable. Opus only.
NEW-ABS-5: Query 5 absence list doesn't guard against session 2/3 high-importance Decision memories. Sonnet rates HIGH.
MEDIUM: GT-3: Query 10 missing Memory[49]. 3/3 confirmed.
GT-4: Query 12 missing Memory[18]. 3/3 confirmed with nuance.
QT-2: Query 9 is temporal_multi_hop. 3/3 confirmed.
ABS-1: Query 9 missing Memory[48] in absence. 3/3 confirmed.
ABS-2: Query 6 missing session-1 memories in absence. 3/3 confirmed.
CARD-1: Expected set size asymmetry. 3/3 confirmed.
NEW-ABSENCE-SCOPE: count_absence_violations checks all 10 results vs top-5 for other metrics. 3/3 found.
NEW-Q6-NARROW: Query 6 expected [15,17,18] too narrow (GT-2 analogue). 3/3 found.
NEW-Q16-STALE: Query 16 expected [11] is stale; Memory[55] more current. 3/3 found.
NEW-Q15-MEMORY59: Query 15 missing Memory[59] (lessons learned). 2/3 found.
NEW-Q5-ABSENCE-GAP: Query 5 absence list doesn't guard session 2/3. 1/3 found (Sonnet) but compelling.
NEW-TYPE-MEMORY9: Memory[9] Task vs Memory[22] Decision inconsistency. 1/3 found (Opus).
NEW-MRR-GAMBLING: Composite rewards rank-1 gambling over broad quality. 2/3 found.
NEW-CORRELATED-MEMORIES: Memory[46,48,18] each in 3 expected sets, creating correlated effects. 1/3 found (Opus).
SRC-PRECISION-K-FIXED-DENOM: Precision@k is fixed-denominator and undocumented. Copilot source audit.
SRC-INDEX-BRITTLENESS: The benchmark contract depends on positional memory indices. Copilot source audit.
SRC-OPEN-DOMAIN-EXACTNESS: Open-domain prompts are graded as narrow exact-set fixtures. Copilot source audit.
LOW: GT-5: Query 13 missing Memory[4]. 3/3 confirmed.
TYPE-1: Memory[5] should be Decision. 3/3 confirmed.
TYPE-2: Memory[38] should be Error. 3/3 confirmed (Haiku initially resisted).
TAG-1: Person-name tags inconsistent. 3/3 confirmed.
TAG-2: Redundant 'decision' tag on Memory[2,3]. 3/3 confirmed.
NEW-Q19-MEMORY51: Query 19 missing Memory[51]. 2/3 found.
NEW-TYPE-MEMORY44: Memory[44] typed Task, better as Observation. 2/3 found.
NEW-CORPUS-INCONSISTENCY: Memory[46] 'back to' PostgreSQL never established. Sonnet only.
NEW-MEMORY21-TYPE: Memory[21] Conversation type but content is a decision. Sonnet only.
NEW-MEMORY53-SINGLETON: Memory[53] only Discovery type in corpus. Opus only.
NEW-Q11-AMBIGUITY: 'Most bugs' attribution ambiguous. Opus only.
NEW-MEMORY48-DECISION: Error-typed but contains explicit decision. Opus only.
NEW-EMPTY-SET: Inconsistent empty-set handling across scoring functions. Sonnet only.
NEW-RECALL-CEILING: Fixing GT-2 creates R@5<=0.5 ceiling. Sonnet only.
NEW-TAG-REVERSED-REVERT: 'reversed' vs 'revert' tag inconsistency. Sonnet round 1.
NEW-SEPARATOR-WIDTH: print_report 78 vs 88 char separator. 2/3 found.
NEW-LATENCY-TRUNCATION: f32→u64 cast loses fractional ms. Opus only.
SRC-TYPE-SUMMARY-NO-VARIANCE: Type-level reporting publishes means without spread. Copilot source audit.
